# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 3

This is turn 3. Turns 1 through 2 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 3.

## Turn 3 — Candidate \(A_1\) is ruled out structurally

I retained the Turn-1 relation
\[
u_i=(x_i\tau)^{\omega_2},\qquad d_i=u_ix_i^{-1},
\]
\[
R_{A_1}=x_0^2x_1^4u_1^{-1}x_1^\sigma d_0d_1^{-1}=1,
\]
with \(\omega_2=40491355905\). 

Rather than continue testing isolated central covers, I tested whether its open subgroups have the mandatory local-duality structure.

### The decisive tame subgroup

Let
\[
H_7=\langle s,t\mid s^3=t^7=1,\ t^s=t^2\rangle
   \cong C_7\!:\!C_3.
\]
The implicit tame relation permits the epimorphism
\[
\sigma\mapsto s,\qquad \tau\mapsto t,\qquad x_0,x_1\mapsto1.
\]
Let \(U\) be its kernel. If \(A_1\) presented \(G_{\mathbf Q_2}\), then \(U\) would be the absolute Galois group of the degree-\(21\) tame Galois extension fixed by this kernel.

Consequently its maximal pro-\(2\) quotient must be Demushkin. In particular,
\[
\dim H^1(U,\mathbf F_2)=21+2=23,\qquad
\dim H^2(U,\mathbf F_2)=1,
\]
and the cup product
\[
H^1(U,\mathbf F_2)\times H^1(U,\mathbf F_2)
   \longrightarrow H^2(U,\mathbf F_2)
\]
must be a perfect pairing of rank \(23\).

### Reidemeister–Schreier/Magnus computation

I performed Reidemeister–Schreier rewriting for this index-\(21\) subgroup.

For the quadratic mod-\(2\) Magnus calculation, \(\omega_2\) may be represented by \(21\), since
\[
21\equiv1\pmod4,\qquad 21\equiv0\pmod7.
\]
The resulting pro-\(2\) presentation has:

\[
64\text{ Schreier generators},\qquad 42\text{ relators}.
\]

Its mod-\(2\) exponent-sum matrix \(L\) has rank \(41\). Thus row-and-column elimination yields a \(23\)-generator, one-relator pro-\(2\) presentation, exactly the expected dimensions:
\[
64-41=23,\qquad 42-41=1.
\]

However, the second Magnus coefficients of the remaining relator give a symmetric \(23\times23\) cup-product matrix of rank

\[
\boxed{17}.
\]

It therefore has a six-dimensional radical. This contradicts local Tate duality, which requires rank \(23\).

Hence:

\[
\boxed{\text{Candidate }A_1\text{ cannot present }G_{\mathbf Q_2}.}
\]

This is independent of the official 5402-group verifier.

### It is a family-wide defect

I repeated the computation for
\[
H_e=C_e:C_{\operatorname{ord}_e(2)}
\]
using the canonical tame action \(t^s=t^2\). “Required” is the perfect local-duality rank \(|H_e|+2\).

| \(e\) | \(|H_e|\) | Required | \(R_0\) rank | \(A_1\) rank |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| 3 | 6 | 8 | 8 | 8 |
| 5 | 20 | 22 | 22 | **14** |
| 7 | 21 | 23 | 23 | **17** |
| 9 | 54 | 56 | 56 | **52** |
| 11 | 110 | 112 | 112 | **92** |
| 13 | 156 | 158 | 158 | **134** |
| 15 | 60 | 62 | 62 | **54** |
| 17 | 136 | 138 | 138 | **106** |

Thus the defect ratio \(d_0d_1^{-1}\) that repairs the \(GL_2(3)\) central obstruction systematically destroys the Poincaré-duality form on nontrivial tame-character components. The failed \(R_0\), conversely, passes every one of these open-subgroup duality tests.

This cleanly separates the two requirements:

\[
\begin{array}{c|c|c}
&GL_2(3)\text{ obstruction}&\text{tame-open duality}\\ \hline
R_0&\text{fails}&\text{passes}\\
A_1&\text{passes}&\text{fails}
\end{array}
\]

### Why more casewise patches are not the answer

I searched corrections of the form
\[
[d_i,z^{\tau^k}],
\qquad
i\in\{0,1\},\quad
z\in\{x_0,x_1,u_0,u_1,d_0,d_1\}.
\]
These corrections vanish when \(\tau=1\), and also vanish in elementary-abelian \(2\)-cores, so they preserve all the Turn-2 linear results.

Some repair individual tame quotients:

- \([d_0,x_0^\tau]\) repairs the \(C_7:C_3\) pairing and preserves the \(GL_2(3)\) count, but leaves the \(C_5:C_4\) rank at \(14\).
- \([d_1,x_1^{\tau^9}]\) repairs the \(e=3,5,7\) pairings and preserves the earlier finite counts, but fails for \(e=9\) and \(e=11\).
- For \(e=9\), none of the \(108\) single atoms in this family restores rank \(56\).
- No product of at most two such atoms that restores the \(e=9\) pairing also retains the required \(384\) epimorphisms onto \(GL_2(3)\).

So the next correction cannot sensibly be selected by patching one tame order at a time. It must preserve a universal group-ring-valued duality form while changing the \(Q_8\)-central obstruction.

The reproducible RS/Magnus certificate is here: [turn3_rs_magnus_certificate.py](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn3_rs_magnus_certificate.py).

**ESTABLISHED**: Candidate \(A_1\) is false. Its index-\(21\) tame kernel has the correct \(H^1\) and \(H^2\) dimensions but a cup-product matrix of rank \(17\) instead of the locally required rank \(23\). The same degeneration occurs uniformly across many tame quotients. The failed candidate \(R_0\) passes all tested tame-open duality checks, pinpointing the incompatibility introduced by \(d_0d_1^{-1}\).

**GAP**: Find a correction to \(R_0\) that changes the first central Frattini obstruction—giving \(384\) epimorphisms onto \(GL_2(3)\)—without changing the perfect cup-product form in every finite tame specialization.

**NEXT**: Compute the universal second Fox derivative over the tame group ring
\[
\mathbf F_2\langle S^{\pm1},T^{\pm1}\rangle/(S^{-1}TS-T^2),
\]
classify corrections that preserve its unimodular pairing under every \(T\)-specialization, and only then impose the \(GL_2(3)\) obstruction condition.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
